text:
There once was a curious little mole that lived under a garden. Every day she would dig tunnels, chasing critters through the dirt. She loved to collect worms and beetle nuts. Her best friend was a shy ladybug named Laurel.

One sunny morning, the mole discovered a glittering gem buried deep within the earth. Excited, she carried it back home and dug a little hole to put it proudly on display. As she admired her shiny discovery, she heard Laurel flying above her head.

"Come join," called Laurel. "It's fascinating here."

Curious, the mole pushed aside some soil to look out. There was a magnificent phoenix bird, its fiery feathers glimmering in the sunlight. The bird saw the gem and swooped down. With a nimble flick of its wings, it plucked the diamond from the little mole's fingers.

The phoenix flew away, leaving the mole looking for her precious stone. Days passed, and the mole searched tirelessly, hopping from burrow
